description_long: This is a vintage box-style television encased in a wood-textured housing with visible wear and aging, including scuffs and faded edges. The front features a convex glass screen, a JVC logo, a rotary dial, and several push buttons and colored knobs, suggesting analog controls. The left side displays two circular speaker grilles and is etched with graffiti-like carvings, including a heart and geometric shapes. The right side is adorned with stickers, such as a 'Pizza Planet' logo, a 'Pirates of Heart' emblem, and a yellow sticky note reading 'TO DO - CLEAN GARAGE.' The top and back are plain, with the top showing significant surface wear and no visible vents or ports. The overall design is bulky and robust, typical of late 20th-century CRT televisions, and the object appears heavy and solid.
description: A vintage, wood-encased CRT television with a convex glass screen, analog controls, graffiti carvings, and stickers on the sides.
description_view_0: Front view shows the convex glass screen, JVC logo, rotary dial, and a cluster of control buttons and knobs.
description_view_1: Left side view reveals two round speaker grilles and several graffiti-like carvings etched into the wood-textured surface.
description_view_2: Top view displays a plain, worn wood-textured surface with visible scuffs and no additional features.
description_view_3: Right side view features several stickers, including 'Pizza Planet,' 'Pirates of Heart,' and a yellow sticky note, with no visible controls.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, black plastic bezel with a matte finish and slightly rounded corners.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of buttons and knobs on the front right panel.
